     246              : /*
     247              :  * We have different sets for client and server message level options because
     248              :  * they sort slightly different (see "log" level), and because "fatal"/"panic"
     249              :  * aren't sensible for client_min_messages.
     250              :  */

     467              : /*
     468              :  * password_encryption used to be a boolean, so accept all the likely
     469              :  * variants of "on", too. "off" used to store passwords in plaintext,
     470              :  * but we don't support that anymore.
     471              :  */
     472              : static const struct config_enum_entry password_encryption_options[] = {
     473              :         {"md5", PASSWORD_TYPE_MD5, false},
     474              :         {"scram-sha-256", PASSWORD_TYPE_SCRAM_SHA_256, false},
     475              :         {"on", PASSWORD_TYPE_MD5, true},
     476              :         {"true", PASSWORD_TYPE_MD5, true},
     477              :         {"yes", PASSWORD_TYPE_MD5, true},
     478              :         {"1", PASSWORD_TYPE_MD5, true},
     479              :         {NULL, 0, false}
     480              : };
     481              : 
     482              : const struct config_enum_entry ssl_protocol_versions_info[] = {
     483              :         {"", PG_TLS_ANY, false},
     484              :         {"TLSv1", PG_TLS1_VERSION, false},
     485              :         {"TLSv1.1", PG_TLS1_1_VERSION, false},
     486              :         {"TLSv1.2", PG_TLS1_2_VERSION, false},
     487              :         {"TLSv1.3", PG_TLS1_3_VERSION, false},
     488              :         {NULL, 0, false}
     489              : };
     490              : 
     491              : StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(ssl_protocol_versions_info) == (PG_TLS1_3_VERSION + 2),
     492              :                                  "array length mismatch");
     493              : 
     494              : static struct config_enum_entry shared_memory_options[] = {
     495              : #ifndef WIN32
     496              :         {"sysv", SHMEM_TYPE_SYSV, false},
     497              : #endif
     498              : #ifndef EXEC_BACKEND
     499              :         {"mmap", SHMEM_TYPE_MMAP, false},
     500              : #endif
     501              : #ifdef WIN32
     502              :         {"windows", SHMEM_TYPE_WINDOWS, false},
     503              : #endif
     504              :         {NULL, 0, false}
     505              : };

     516              : /*
     517              :  * GUC option variables that are exported from this module
     518              :  */
     519              : bool            log_duration = false;
     520              : bool            Debug_print_plan = false;
     521              : bool            Debug_print_parse = false;
     522              : bool            Debug_print_rewritten = false;
     523              : bool            Debug_pretty_print = true;
     524              : 
     525              : bool            log_parser_stats = false;
     526              : bool            log_planner_stats = false;
     527              : bool            log_executor_stats = false;
     528              : bool            log_statement_stats = false;    /* this is sort of all three above
     529              :                                                                                          * together */
     530              : bool            log_btree_build_stats = false;
     531              : char       *event_source;
     532              : 
     533              : bool            row_security;
     534              : bool            check_function_bodies = true;
     535              : 
     536              : /*
     537              :  * This GUC exists solely for backward compatibility, check its definition for
     538              :  * details.
     539              :  */
     540              : bool            default_with_oids = false;
     541              : bool            session_auth_is_superuser;
     542              : 
     543              : int                     log_min_error_statement = ERROR;
     544              : int                     log_min_messages = WARNING;
     545              : int                     client_min_messages = NOTICE;
     546              : int                     log_min_duration_sample = -1;
     547              : int                     log_min_duration_statement = -1;
     548              : int                     log_parameter_max_length = -1;
     549              : int                     log_parameter_max_length_on_error = 0;
     550              : int                     log_temp_files = -1;
     551              : double          log_statement_sample_rate = 1.0;
     552              : double          log_xact_sample_rate = 0;
     553              : int                     trace_recovery_messages = LOG;
     554              : char       *backtrace_functions;
     555              : char       *backtrace_symbol_list;
     556              : 
     557              : int                     temp_file_limit = -1;
     558              : 
     559              : int                     num_temp_buffers = 1024;

     561              : char       *cluster_name = "";
     562              : char       *ConfigFileName;
     563              : char       *HbaFileName;
     564              : char       *IdentFileName;
     565              : char       *external_pid_file;

     575              : 
     576              : /*
     577              :  * SSL renegotiation was been removed in PostgreSQL 9.5, but we tolerate it
     578              :  * being set to zero (meaning never renegotiate) for backward compatibility.
     579              :  * This avoids breaking compatibility with clients that have never supported
     580              :  * renegotiation and therefore always try to zero it.
     581              :  */
     582              : int                     ssl_renegotiation_limit;
     583              : 
     584              : /*
     585              :  * This really belongs in pg_shmem.c, but is defined here so that it doesn't
     586              :  * need to be duplicated in all the different implementations of pg_shmem.c.
     587              :  */
     588              : int                     huge_pages;
     589              : 
     590              : /*
     591              :  * These variables are all dummies that don't do anything, except in some
     592              :  * cases provide the value for SHOW to display.  The real state is elsewhere
     593              :  * and is kept in sync by assign_hooks.
     594              :  */

     596              : static double phony_random_seed;
     597              : static char *client_encoding_string;
     598              : static char *datestyle_string;
     599              : static char *locale_collate;
     600              : static char *locale_ctype;
     601              : static char *server_encoding_string;
     602              : static char *server_version_string;
     603              : static int      server_version_num;
     604              : static char *timezone_string;
     605              : static char *log_timezone_string;
     606              : static char *timezone_abbreviations_string;
     607              : static char *data_directory;
     608              : static char *session_authorization_string;
     609              : static int      max_function_args;
     610              : static int      max_index_keys;
     611              : static int      max_identifier_length;
     612              : static int      block_size;
     613              : static int      segment_size;
     614              : static int      wal_block_size;
     615              : static bool data_checksums;
     616              : static bool integer_datetimes;
     617              : static bool assert_enabled;
     618              : static char *recovery_target_timeline_string;

     620              : static char *recovery_target_xid_string;
     621              : static char *recovery_target_name_string;
     622              : static char *recovery_target_lsn_string;
     623              : static char *restrict_nonsystem_relation_kind_string;

     659              :          /* PGC_S_FILE */ "configuration file",

     661              :          /* PGC_S_GLOBAL */ "global",
     662              :          /* PGC_S_DATABASE */ "database",
     663              :          /* PGC_S_USER */ "user",
     664              :          /* PGC_S_DATABASE_USER */ "database user",
     665              :          /* PGC_S_CLIENT */ "client",
     666              :          /* PGC_S_OVERRIDE */ "override",

     682              :         /* FILE_LOCATIONS */
     683              :         gettext_noop("File Locations"),
     684              :         /* CONN_AUTH */
     685              :         gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication"),
     686              :         /* CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS */
     687              :         gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / Connection Settings"),
     688              :         /* CONN_AUTH_AUTH */
     689              :         gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / Authentication"),
     690              :         /* CONN_AUTH_SSL */
     691              :         gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / SSL"),
     692              :         /* RESOURCES */
     693              :         gettext_noop("Resource Usage"),
     694              :         /* RESOURCES_MEM */
     695              :         gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Memory"),
     696              :         /* RESOURCES_DISK */
     697              :         gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Disk"),
     698              :         /* RESOURCES_KERNEL */

     700              :         /* RESOURCES_VACUUM_DELAY */

     704              :         /* RESOURCES_ASYNCHRONOUS */
     705              :         gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Asynchronous Behavior"),
     706              :         /* WAL */

     708              :         /* WAL_SETTINGS */

     710              :         /* WAL_CHECKPOINTS */

     788              : 
     789              : /*
     790              :  * Displayable names for GUC variable types (enum config_type)
     791              :  *
     792              :  * Note: these strings are deliberately not localized.
     793              :  */
     794              : const char *const config_type_names[] =
     795              : {
     796              :          /* PGC_BOOL */ "bool",
     797              :          /* PGC_INT */ "integer",
     798              :          /* PGC_REAL */ "real",
     799              :          /* PGC_STRING */ "string",
     800              :          /* PGC_ENUM */ "enum"
     801              : };
     802              : 
     803              : StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(config_type_names) == (PGC_ENUM + 1),
     804              :                                  "array length mismatch");
     805              : 
     806              : /*
     807              :  * Unit conversion tables.
     808              :  *
     809              :  * There are two tables, one for memory units, and another for time units.
     810              :  * For each supported conversion from one unit to another, we have an entry
     811              :  * in the table.
     812              :  *
     813              :  * To keep things simple, and to avoid possible roundoff error,
     814              :  * conversions are never chained.  There needs to be a direct conversion
     815              :  * between all units (of the same type).
     816              :  *
     817              :  * The conversions for each base unit must be kept in order from greatest to
     818              :  * smallest human-friendly unit; convert_xxx_from_base_unit() rely on that.
     819              :  * (The order of the base-unit groups does not matter.)
     820              :  */
     821              : #define MAX_UNIT_LEN            3       /* length of longest recognized unit string */
     822              : 
     823              : typedef struct
     824              : {
     825              :         char            unit[MAX_UNIT_LEN + 1]; /* unit, as a string, like "kB" or
     826              :                                                                                  * "min" */
     827              :         int                     base_unit;              /* GUC_UNIT_XXX */
     828              :         double          multiplier;             /* Factor for converting unit -> base_unit */
     829              : } unit_conversion;
     830              : 
     831              : /* Ensure that the constants in the tables don't overflow or underflow */
     832              : #if BLCKSZ < 1024 || BLCKSZ > (1024*1024)
     833              : #error BLCKSZ must be between 1KB and 1MB
     834              : #endif
     835              : #if XLOG_BLCKSZ < 1024 || XLOG_BLCKSZ > (1024*1024)
     836              : #error XLOG_BLCKSZ must be between 1KB and 1MB
     837              : #endif
     838              : 
     839              : static const char *memory_units_hint = gettext_noop("Valid units for this parameter are \"B\", \"kB\", \"MB\", \"GB\", and \"TB\".");
     840              : 
     841              : static const unit_conversion memory_unit_conversion_table[] =
     842              : {
     843              :         {"TB", GUC_UNIT_BYTE, 1024.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0},
     844              :         {"GB", GUC_UNIT_BYTE, 1024.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0},
     845              :         {"MB", GUC_UNIT_BYTE, 1024.0 * 1024.0},
     846              :         {"kB", GUC_UNIT_BYTE, 1024.0},
     847              :         {"B", GUC_UNIT_BYTE, 1.0},
     848              : 
     849              :         {"TB", GUC_UNIT_KB, 1024.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0},
     850              :         {"GB", GUC_UNIT_KB, 1024.0 * 1024.0},
     851              :         {"MB", GUC_UNIT_KB, 1024.0},
     852              :         {"kB", GUC_UNIT_KB, 1.0},
     853              :         {"B", GUC_UNIT_KB, 1.0 / 1024.0},
     854              : 
     855              :         {"TB", GUC_UNIT_MB, 1024.0 * 1024.0},
     856              :         {"GB", GUC_UNIT_MB, 1024.0},
     857              :         {"MB", GUC_UNIT_MB, 1.0},
     858              :         {"kB", GUC_UNIT_MB, 1.0 / 1024.0},
     859              :         {"B", GUC_UNIT_MB, 1.0 / (1024.0 * 1024.0)},
     860              : 
     861              :         {"TB", GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS, (1024.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0) / (BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     862              :         {"GB", GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS, (1024.0 * 1024.0) / (BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     863              :         {"MB", GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS, 1024.0 / (BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     864              :         {"kB", GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS, 1.0 / (BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     865              :         {"B", GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS, 1.0 / BLCKSZ},
     866              : 
     867              :         {"TB", GUC_UNIT_XBLOCKS, (1024.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0) / (XLOG_BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     868              :         {"GB", GUC_UNIT_XBLOCKS, (1024.0 * 1024.0) / (XLOG_BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     869              :         {"MB", GUC_UNIT_XBLOCKS, 1024.0 / (XLOG_BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     870              :         {"kB", GUC_UNIT_XBLOCKS, 1.0 / (XLOG_BLCKSZ / 1024)},
     871              :         {"B", GUC_UNIT_XBLOCKS, 1.0 / XLOG_BLCKSZ},
     872              : 
     873              :         {""}                                          /* end of table marker */
     874              : };
     875              : 
     876              : static const char *time_units_hint = gettext_noop("Valid units for this parameter are \"us\", \"ms\", \"s\", \"min\", \"h\", and \"d\".");
     877              : 
     878              : static const unit_conversion time_unit_conversion_table[] =
     879              : {
     880              :         {"d", GUC_UNIT_MS, 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24},
     881              :         {"h", GUC_UNIT_MS, 1000 * 60 * 60},
     882              :         {"min", GUC_UNIT_MS, 1000 * 60},
     883              :         {"s", GUC_UNIT_MS, 1000},
     884              :         {"ms", GUC_UNIT_MS, 1},
     885              :         {"us", GUC_UNIT_MS, 1.0 / 1000},
     886              : 
     887              :         {"d", GUC_UNIT_S, 60 * 60 * 24},
     888              :         {"h", GUC_UNIT_S, 60 * 60},
     889              :         {"min", GUC_UNIT_S, 60},
     890              :         {"s", GUC_UNIT_S, 1},
     891              :         {"ms", GUC_UNIT_S, 1.0 / 1000},
     892              :         {"us", GUC_UNIT_S, 1.0 / (1000 * 1000)},
     893              : 
     894              :         {"d", GUC_UNIT_MIN, 60 * 24},
     895              :         {"h", GUC_UNIT_MIN, 60},
     896              :         {"min", GUC_UNIT_MIN, 1},
     897              :         {"s", GUC_UNIT_MIN, 1.0 / 60},
     898              :         {"ms", GUC_UNIT_MIN, 1.0 / (1000 * 60)},
     899              :         {"us", GUC_UNIT_MIN, 1.0 / (1000 * 1000 * 60)},
     900              : 
     901              :         {""}                                          /* end of table marker */
     902              : };
     903              : 
     904              : /*
     905              :  * Contents of GUC tables
     906              :  *
     907              :  * See src/backend/utils/misc/README for design notes.
     908              :  *
     909              :  * TO ADD AN OPTION:
     910              :  *
     911              :  * 1. Declare a global variable of type bool, int, double, or char*
     912              :  *        and make use of it.
     913              :  *
     914              :  * 2. Decide at what times it's safe to set the option. See guc.h for
     915              :  *        details.
     916              :  *
     917              :  * 3. Decide on a name, a default value, upper and lower bounds (if
     918              :  *        applicable), etc.
     919              :  *
     920              :  * 4. Add a record below.
     921              :  *
     922              :  * 5. Add it to src/backend/utils/misc/postgresql.conf.sample, if
     923              :  *        appropriate.
     924              :  *
     925              :  * 6. Don't forget to document the option (at least in config.sgml).
     926              :  *
     927              :  * 7. If it's a new GUC_LIST_QUOTE option, you must add it to
     928              :  *        variable_is_guc_list_quote() in src/bin/pg_dump/dumputils.c.
     929              :  */
     930              : 
     931              : 
     932              : /******** option records follow ********/
     933              : 
     934              : static struct config_bool ConfigureNamesBool[] =
     935              : {
     936              :         {
     937              :                 {"enable_seqscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
     938              :                         gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of sequential-scan plans."),
     939              :                         NULL,
     940              :                         GUC_EXPLAIN
     941              :                 },
     942              :                 &enable_seqscan,
     943              :                 true,
     944              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
     945              :         },
     946              :         {
     947              :                 {"enable_indexscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
     948              :                         gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of index-scan plans."),
     949              :                         NULL,
     950              :                         GUC_EXPLAIN
     951              :                 },
     952              :                 &enable_indexscan,
     953              :                 true,
     954              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
     955              :         },
     956              :         {
     957              :                 {"enable_indexonlyscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
     958              :                         gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of index-only-scan plans."),
     959              :                         NULL,
     960              :                         GUC_EXPLAIN
     961              :                 },
     962              :                 &enable_indexonlyscan,
     963              :                 true,
     964              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
     965              :         },
     966              :         {
     967              :                 {"enable_bitmapscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,

    1167              :                 {"ssl_passphrase_command_supports_reload", PGC_SIGHUP, CONN_AUTH_SSL,

    1173              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1174              :         },
    1175              :         {
    1176              :                 {"ssl_prefer_server_ciphers", PGC_SIGHUP, CONN_AUTH_SSL,
    1177              :                         gettext_noop("Give priority to server ciphersuite order."),
    1178              :                         NULL
    1179              :                 },
    1180              :                 &SSLPreferServerCiphers,
    1181              :                 true,
    1182              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1183              :         },
    1184              :         {
    1185              :                 {"fsync", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_SETTINGS,
    1186              :                         gettext_noop("Forces synchronization of updates to disk."),
    1187              :                         gettext_noop("The server will use the fsync() system call in several places to make "
    1188              :                                                  "sure that updates are physically written to disk. This ensures "
    1189              :                                                  "that a database cluster will recover to a consistent state after "
    1190              :                                                  "an operating system or hardware crash.")
    1191              :                 },
    1192              :                 &enableFsync,
    1193              :                 true,
    1194              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1195              :         },
    1196              :         {
    1197              :                 {"ignore_checksum_failure", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
    1198              :                         gettext_noop("Continues processing after a checksum failure."),
    1199              :                         gettext_noop("Detection of a checksum failure normally causes PostgreSQL to "
    1200              :                                                  "report an error, aborting the current transaction. Setting "
    1201              :                                                  "ignore_checksum_failure to true causes the system to ignore the failure "
    1202              :                                                  "(but still report a warning), and continue processing. This "
    1203              :                                                  "behavior could cause crashes or other serious problems. Only "
    1204              :                                                  "has an effect if checksums are enabled."),
    1205              :                         GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
    1206              :                 },
    1207              :                 &ignore_checksum_failure,
    1208              :                 false,
    1209              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1210              :         },
    1211              :         {
    1212              :                 {"zero_damaged_pages", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
    1213              :                         gettext_noop("Continues processing past damaged page headers."),
    1214              :                         gettext_noop("Detection of a damaged page header normally causes PostgreSQL to "
    1215              :                                                  "report an error, aborting the current transaction. Setting "
    1216              :                                                  "zero_damaged_pages to true causes the system to instead report a "
    1217              :                                                  "warning, zero out the damaged page, and continue processing. This "
    1218              :                                                  "behavior will destroy data, namely all the rows on the damaged page."),
    1219              :                         GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
    1220              :                 },
    1221              :                 &zero_damaged_pages,
    1222              :                 false,
    1223              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1224              :         },
    1225              :         {
    1226              :                 {"ignore_invalid_pages", PGC_POSTMASTER, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
    1227              :                         gettext_noop("Continues recovery after an invalid pages failure."),
    1228              :                         gettext_noop("Detection of WAL records having references to "
    1229              :                                                  "invalid pages during recovery causes PostgreSQL to "
    1230              :                                                  "raise a PANIC-level error, aborting the recovery. "
    1231              :                                                  "Setting ignore_invalid_pages to true causes "
    1232              :                                                  "the system to ignore invalid page references "
    1233              :                                                  "in WAL records (but still report a warning), "
    1234              :                                                  "and continue recovery. This behavior may cause "
    1235              :                                                  "crashes, data loss, propagate or hide corruption, "
    1236              :                                                  "or other serious problems. Only has an effect "
    1237              :                                                  "during recovery or in standby mode."),
    1238              :                         GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
    1239              :                 },
    1240              :                 &ignore_invalid_pages,
    1241              :                 false,
    1242              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1243              :         },
    1244              :         {
    1245              :                 {"full_page_writes", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_SETTINGS,
    1246              :                         gettext_noop("Writes full pages to WAL when first modified after a checkpoint."),
    1247              :                         gettext_noop("A page write in process during an operating system crash might be "
    1248              :                                                  "only partially written to disk.  During recovery, the row changes "
    1249              :                                                  "stored in WAL are not enough to recover.  This option writes "
    1250              :                                                  "pages when first modified after a checkpoint to WAL so full recovery "
    1251              :                                                  "is possible.")
    1252              :                 },
    1253              :                 &fullPageWrites,
    1254              :                 true,
    1255              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1256              :         },
    1257              :

    1580              :         {
    1581              :                 {"log_hostname", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHAT,
    1582              :                         gettext_noop("Logs the host name in the connection logs."),
    1583              :                         gettext_noop("By default, connection logs only show the IP address "
    1584              :                                                  "of the connecting host. If you want them to show the host name you "
    1585              :                                                  "can turn this on, but depending on your host name resolution "
    1586              :                                                  "setup it might impose a non-negligible performance penalty.")
    1587              :                 },
    1588              :                 &log_hostname,
    1589              :                 false,
    1590              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1591              :         },
    1592              :         {
    1593              :                 {"transform_null_equals", PGC_USERSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_CLIENT,
    1594              :                         gettext_noop("Treats \"expr=NULL\" as \"expr IS NULL\"."),
    1595              :                         gettext_noop("When turned on, expressions of the form expr = NULL "
    1596              :                                                  "(or NULL = expr) are treated as expr IS NULL, that is, they "
    1597              :                                                  "return true if expr evaluates to the null value, and false "
    1598              :                                                  "otherwise. The correct behavior of expr = NULL is to always "
    1599              :                                                  "return null (unknown).")
    1600              :                 },
    1601              :                 &Transform_null_equals,
    1602              :                 false,
    1603              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    1604              :         },

    1656              :                 },

    1659              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL

    1681              : 
    1682              :         /*
    1683              :          * WITH OIDS support, and consequently default_with_oids, was removed in
    1684              :          * PostgreSQL 12, but we tolerate the parameter being set to false to
    1685              :          * avoid unnecessarily breaking older dump files.
    1686              :          */
    1687              :         {
    1688              :                 {"default_with_oids", PGC_USERSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_PREVIOUS,

    1692              :                 },
    1693              :                 &default_with_oids,
    1694              :                 false,

    1696              :         },

    2062              : 
    2063              : static struct config_int ConfigureNamesInt[] =
    2064              : {
    2065              :         {
    2066              :                 {"archive_timeout", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_ARCHIVING,
    2067              :                         gettext_noop("Forces a switch to the next WAL file if a "
    2068              :                                                  "new file has not been started within N seconds."),
    2069              :                         NULL,
    2070              :                         GUC_UNIT_S
    2071              :                 },
    2072              :                 &XLogArchiveTimeout,
    2073              :                 0, 0, INT_MAX / 2,
    2074              :                 NULL, NULL, NULL
    2075              :         },
    2076              :         {
    2077              :                 {"post_auth_delay", PGC_BACKEND, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
    2078              :                         gettext_noop("Waits N seconds on connection startup after authentication."),
    2079              :                         gettext_noop("This allows attaching a debugger to the process."),

    2591              :                         gettext_noop("The shared predicate lock table is sized on the assumption that "

    3614              :

    3644              :

    3656              :                 {"log_statement_sample_rate", PGC_SUSET, LOGGING_WHEN,

    3661              :                 1.0, 0.0, 1.0,

    3681              : };

    5075              : 
    5076              : /*
    5077              :  * Build the sorted array.  This is split out so that it could be

    5081              : void
    5082            5 : build_guc_variables(void)

    5090              :         {
    5091          485 :                 struct config_bool *conf = &ConfigureNamesBool[i];
    5092              : 
    5093              :                 /* Rather than requiring vartype to be filled in by hand, do this: */
    5094          485 :                 conf->gen.vartype = PGC_BOOL;
    5095          485 :                 num_vars++;
    5096              :         }
    5097              : 
    5098          580 :         for (i = 0; ConfigureNamesInt[i].gen.name; i++)
    5099              :         {
    5100          575 :                 struct config_int *conf = &ConfigureNamesInt[i];
    5101              : 
    5102          575 :                 conf->gen.vartype = PGC_INT;
    5103          575 :                 num_vars++;
    5104              :         }
    5105              : 
    5106          130 :         for (i = 0; ConfigureNamesReal[i].gen.name; i++)
    5107              :         {
    5108          125 :                 struct config_real *conf = &ConfigureNamesReal[i];

    5110          125 :                 conf->gen.vartype = PGC_REAL;

    5112              :         }
    5113              : 
    5114          355 :         for (i = 0; ConfigureNamesString[i].gen.name; i++)

    5118          350 :                 conf->gen.vartype = PGC_STRING;

    5121              : 
    5122          155 :         for (i = 0; ConfigureNamesEnum[i].gen.name; i++)

    5129              : 
    5130              :         /*
    5131              :          * Create table with 20% slack

    5165              :  * Add a new GUC variable to the list of known variables. The

    5168              : static bool
    5169           10 : add_guc_variable(struct config_generic *var, int elevel)

    5249              : /*

    5300              : /*
    5301              :  * comparator for qsorting and bsearching guc_variables array

    5304        52575 : guc_var_compare(const void *a, const void *b)
    5305              : {

    5312              : /*
    5313              :  * the bare comparison function for GUC names
    5314              :  */
    5315              : static int
    5316        52842 : guc_name_compare(const char *namea, const char *nameb)
    5317              : {
    5318              :         /*
    5319              :          * The temptation to use strcasecmp() here must be resisted, because the
    5320              :          * array ordering has to remain stable across setlocale() calls. So, build
    5321              :          * our own with a simple ASCII-only downcasing.
    5322              :          */
    5323       224120 :         while (*namea && *nameb)
    5324              :         {
    5325       222653 :                 char            cha = *namea++;
    5326       222653 :                 char            chb = *nameb++;
    5327              : 
    5328       222653 :                 if (cha >= 'A' && cha <= 'Z')
    5329          439 :                         cha += 'a' - 'A';
    5330       222653 :                 if (chb >= 'A' && chb <= 'Z')
    5331          343 :                         chb += 'a' - 'A';
    5332       222653 :                 if (cha != chb)
    5333        51375 :                         return cha - chb;
    5334              :         }
    5335         1467 :         if (*namea)
    5336          215 :                 return 1;                               /* a is longer */
    5337         1252 :         if (*nameb)
    5338          846 :                 return -1;                              /* b is longer */
    5339          406 :         return 0;
    5340              : }

    5343              : /*
    5344              :  * Initialize GUC options during program startup.

    5350            5 : InitializeGUCOptions(void)

    5359              :

    5364              :

    5370              :         {

    5372              :         }
    5373              : 
    5374            5 :         guc_dirty = false;
    5375              : 
    5376            5 :         reporting_enabled = false;
    5377              : 
    5378              :         /*
    5379              :          * Prevent any attempt to override the transaction modes from
    5380              :          * non-interactive sources.
    5381              :          */
    5382            5 :         SetConfigOption("transaction_isolation", "read committed",
    5383              :                                         PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
    5384            5 :         SetConfigOption("transaction_read_only", "no",
    5385              :                                         PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
    5386            5 :         SetConfigOption("transaction_deferrable", "no",
    5387              :                                         PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
    5388              : 
    5389              :         /*
    5390              :          * For historical reasons, some GUC parameters can receive defaults from

    5392              :          */
    5393            5 :         InitializeGUCOptionsFromEnvironment();
    5394            5 : }
    5395              : 
    5396              : /*
    5397              :  * Assign any GUC values that can come from the server's environment.
    5398              :  *
    5399              :  * This is called from InitializeGUCOptions, and also from ProcessConfigFile
    5400              :  * to deal with the possibility that a setting has been removed from
    5401              :  * postgresql.conf and should now get a value from the environment.

    5405              : static void
    5406            5 : InitializeGUCOptionsFromEnvironment(void)
    5407              : {

    5409              :         long            stack_rlimit;
    5410              : 
    5411            5 :         env = getenv("PGPORT");

    5413            0 :                 SetConfigOption("port", env, PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ENV_VAR);
    5414              : 
    5415            5 :         env = getenv("PGDATESTYLE");

    5417            0 :                 SetConfigOption("datestyle", env, PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ENV_VAR);
    5418              : 
    5419            5 :         env = getenv("PGCLIENTENCODING");

    5421            0 :                 SetConfigOption("client_encoding", env, PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ENV_VAR);
    5422              : 
    5423              :         /*

    5430              :         {
    5431            5 :                 long            new_limit = (stack_rlimit - STACK_DEPTH_SLOP) / 1024L;
    5432              : 
    5433            5 :                 if (new_limit > 100)
    5434              :                 {
    5435              :                         char            limbuf[16];
    5436              : 
    5437            5 :                         new_limit = Min(new_limit, 2048);
    5438            5 :                         sprintf(limbuf, "%ld", new_limit);
    5439            5 :                         SetConfigOption("max_stack_depth", limbuf,
    5440              :                                                         PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ENV_VAR);
    5441              :                 }
    5442              :         }
    5443            5 : }

    5560              : /*

    5952              : 
    5953              : /*
    5954              :  * Do GUC processing at transaction or subtransaction commit or abort, or

    6739              : /*
    6740              :  * Parse and validate a proposed value for the specified configuration
    6741              :  * parameter.
    6742              :  *
    6743              :  * This does built-in checks (such as range limits for an integer parameter)
    6744              :  * and also calls any check hook the parameter may have.
    6745              :  *
    6746              :  * record: GUC variable's info record
    6747              :  * name: variable name (should match the record of course)
    6748              :  * value: proposed value, as a string

    6751              :  * newval: on success, converted parameter value is returned here
    6752              :  * newextra: on success, receives any "extra" data returned by check hook
    6753              :  *      (caller must initialize *newextra to NULL)
    6754              :  *
    6755              :  * Returns true if OK, false if not (or throws error, if elevel >= ERROR)
    6756              :  */

    6758          260 : parse_and_validate_value(struct config_generic *record,
    6759              :                                                  const char *name, const char *value,

    6761              :                                                  union config_var_val *newval, void **newextra)
    6762              : {
    6763          260 :         switch (record->vartype)
    6764              :         {
    6765           68 :                 case PGC_BOOL:
    6766              :                         {

    6918              : 
    6919              : /*
    6920              :  * Sets option `name' to given value.
    6921              :  *
    6922              :  * The value should be a string, which will be parsed and converted to
    6923              :  * the appropriate data type.  The context and source parameters indicate
    6924              :  * in which context this function is being called, so that it can apply the
    6925              :  * access restrictions properly.
    6926              :  *
    6927              :  * If value is NULL, set the option to its default value (normally the
    6928              :  * reset_val, but if source == PGC_S_DEFAULT we instead use the boot_val).
    6929              :  *
    6930              :  * action indicates whether to set the value globally in the session, locally
    6931              :  * to the current top transaction, or just for the duration of a function call.
    6932              :  *
    6933              :  * If changeVal is false then don't really set the option but do all
    6934              :  * the checks to see if it would work.
    6935              :  *
    6936              :  * elevel should normally be passed as zero, allowing this function to make
    6937              :  * its standard choice of ereport level.  However some callers need to be
    6938              :  * able to override that choice; they should pass the ereport level to use.
    6939              :  *
    6940              :  * Return value:
    6941              :  *      +1: the value is valid and was successfully applied.
    6942              :  *      0:      the name or value is invalid, or it's invalid to try to set
    6943              :  *              this GUC now; but elevel was less than ERROR (see below).
    6944              :  *      -1: no error detected, but the value was not applied, either
    6945              :  *              because changeVal is false or there is some overriding setting.
    6946              :  *
    6947              :  * If there is an error (non-existing option, invalid value, etc) then an
    6948              :  * ereport(ERROR) is thrown *unless* this is called for a source for which
    6949              :  * we don't want an ERROR (currently, those are defaults, the config file,
    6950              :  * and per-database or per-user settings, as well as callers who specify
    6951              :  * a less-than-ERROR elevel).  In those cases we write a suitable error
    6952              :  * message via ereport() and return 0.
    6953              :  *
    6954              :  * See also SetConfigOption for an external interface.
    6955              :  */

    6957          283 : set_config_option(const char *name, const char *value,
    6958              :                                   GucContext context, GucSource source,
    6959              :                                   GucAction action, bool changeVal, int elevel,
    6960              :                                   bool is_reload)
    6961              : {

    6963              :         union config_var_val newval_union;

    6967              : 
    6968          283 :         if (elevel == 0)
    6969              :         {
    6970          280 :                 if (source == PGC_S_DEFAULT || source == PGC_S_FILE)
    6971              :                 {
    6972              :                         /*
    6973              :                          * To avoid cluttering the log, only the postmaster bleats loudly
    6974              :                          * about problems with the config file.
    6975              :                          */
    6976           41 :                         elevel = IsUnderPostmaster ? DEBUG3 : LOG;
    6977              :                 }
    6978          239 :                 else if (source == PGC_S_GLOBAL ||
    6979          239 :                                  source == PGC_S_DATABASE ||
    6980          239 :                                  source == PGC_S_USER ||
    6981              :                                  source == PGC_S_DATABASE_USER)
    6982            0 :                         elevel = WARNING;
    6983              :                 else
    6984          239 :                         elevel = ERROR;
    6985              :         }

    6990            0 :                 ereport(elevel,

    6994              :         }
    6995              : 
    6996              :         /*
    6997              :          * GUC_ACTION_SAVE changes are acceptable during a parallel operation,
    6998              :          * because the current worker will also pop the change.  We're probably
    6999              :          * dealing with a function having a proconfig entry.  Only the function's
    7000              :          * body should observe the change, and peer workers do not share in the
    7001              :          * execution of a function call started by this worker.

    7065              :

    7084              :                         /* FALLTHROUGH */
    7085              :                 case PGC_BACKEND:
    7086            0 :                         if (context == PGC_SIGHUP)
    7087              :                         {
    7088              :                                 /*

    7140              :         /*
    7141              :          * Disallow changing GUC_NOT_WHILE_SEC_REST values if we are inside a
    7142              :          * security restriction context.  We can reject this regardless of the GUC
    7143              :          * context or source, mainly because sources that it might be reasonable
    7144              :          * to override for won't be seen while inside a function.
    7145              :          *
    7146              :          * Note: variables marked GUC_NOT_WHILE_SEC_REST should usually be marked
    7147              :          * GUC_NO_RESET_ALL as well, because ResetAllOptions() doesn't check this.
    7148              :          * An exception might be made if the reset value is assumed to be "safe".
    7149              :          *
    7150              :          * Note: this flag is currently used for "session_authorization" and
    7151              :          * "role".  We need to prohibit changing these inside a local userid
    7152              :          * context because when we exit it, GUC won't be notified, leaving things
    7153              :          * out of sync.  (This could be fixed by forcing a new GUC nesting level,
    7154              :          * but that would change behavior in possibly-undesirable ways.)  Also, we
    7155              :          * prohibit changing these in a security-restricted operation because
    7156              :          * otherwise RESET could be used to regain the session user's privileges.
    7157              :          */

    7165              :                          */

    7167              :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),

    7169              :                                                         name)));

    7171              :                 }

    7584              : 
    7585              :                                         /*
    7586              :                                          * Ugly hack: during SET session_authorization, forcibly
    7587              :                                          * do SET ROLE NONE with the same context/source/etc, so
    7588              :                                          * that the effects will have identical lifespan.  This is
    7589              :                                          * required by the SQL spec, and it's not possible to do
    7590              :                                          * it within the variable's check hook or assign hook
    7591              :                                          * because our APIs for those don't pass enough info.
    7592              :                                          * However, don't do it if is_reload: in that case we
    7593              :                                          * expect that if "role" isn't supposed to be default, it
    7594              :                                          * has been or will be set by a separate reload action.
    7595              :                                          *
    7596              :                                          * Also, for the call from InitializeSessionUserId with
    7597              :                                          * source == PGC_S_OVERRIDE, use PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT for
    7598              :                                          * "role"'s source, so that it's still possible to set
    7599              :                                          * "role" from pg_db_role_setting entries.  (See notes in
    7600              :                                          * InitializeSessionUserId before changing this.)
    7601              :                                          *
    7602              :                                          * A fine point: for RESET session_authorization, we do
    7603              :                                          * "RESET role" not "SET ROLE NONE" (by passing down NULL
    7604              :                                          * rather than "none" for the value).  This would have the
    7605              :                                          * same effects in typical cases, but if the reset value
    7606              :                                          * of "role" is not "none" it seems better to revert to
    7607              :                                          * that.
    7608              :                                          */
    7609           98 :                                         if (!is_reload &&
    7610           98 :                                                 strcmp(conf->gen.name, "session_authorization") == 0)
    7611            3 :                                                 (void) set_config_option("role",
    7612              :                                                                                                  value ? "none" : NULL,
    7613              :                                                                                                  orig_context,
    7614              :                                                                                                  (orig_source == PGC_S_OVERRIDE)
    7615              :                                                                                                  ? PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT
    7616              :                                                                                                  : orig_source,
    7617              :                                                                                                  action,
    7618              :                                                                                                  true,
    7619              :                                                                                                  elevel,
    7620              :                                                                                                  false);
    7621              :                                 }

    7947              : /*
    7948              :  * flatten_set_variable_args

    8206              : /*
    8207              :  * Execute ALTER SYSTEM statement.
    8208              :  *
    8209              :  * Read the old PG_AUTOCONF_FILENAME file, merge in the new variable value,
    8210              :  * and write out an updated file.  If the command is ALTER SYSTEM RESET ALL,
    8211              :  * we can skip reading the old file and just write an empty file.
    8212              :  *
    8213              :  * An LWLock is used to serialize updates of the configuration file.
    8214              :  *

    8216              :  * configuration file (PG_AUTOCONF_FILENAME) intact.
    8217              :  */

    8219            0 : AlterSystemSetConfigFile(AlterSystemStmt *altersysstmt)

    8570              : /*

    8640              : /*
    8641              :  * Common code for DefineCustomXXXVariable subroutines: allocate the

    8645           10 : init_custom_variable(const char *name,

    8647              :                                          const char *long_desc,
    8648              :                                          GucContext context,
    8649              :                                          int flags,
    8650              :                                          enum config_type type,
    8651              :                                          size_t sz)
    8652              : {
    8653              :         struct config_generic *gen;
    8654              : 
    8655              :         /*
    8656              :          * Only allow custom PGC_POSTMASTER variables to be created during shared
    8657              :          * library preload; any later than that, we can't ensure that the value

    8659              :          * erroring out isn't safe because we don't know what the calling loadable
    8660              :          * module might already have hooked into.
    8661              :          */
    8662           10 :         if (context == PGC_POSTMASTER &&
    8663            0 :                 !process_shared_preload_libraries_in_progress)
    8664            0 :                 elog(FATAL, "cannot create PGC_POSTMASTER variables after startup");
    8665              : 
    8666              :         /*
    8667              :          * We can't support custom GUC_LIST_QUOTE variables, because the wrong
    8668              :          * things would happen if such a variable were set or pg_dump'd when the
    8669              :          * defining extension isn't loaded.  Again, treat this as fatal because
    8670              :          * the loadable module may be partly initialized already.
    8671              :          */
    8672           10 :         if (flags & GUC_LIST_QUOTE)
    8673            0 :                 elog(FATAL, "extensions cannot define GUC_LIST_QUOTE variables");
    8674              : 
    8675              :         /*
    8676              :          * Before pljava commit 398f3b876ed402bdaec8bc804f29e2be95c75139
    8677              :          * (2015-12-15), two of that module's PGC_USERSET variables facilitated
    8678              :          * trivial escalation to superuser privileges.  Restrict the variables to
    8679              :          * protect sites that have yet to upgrade pljava.
    8680              :          */

    8682            8 :                 (strcmp(name, "pljava.classpath") == 0 ||
    8683            8 :                  strcmp(name, "pljava.vmoptions") == 0))

    8686           10 :         gen = (struct config_generic *) guc_malloc(ERROR, sz);
    8687           10 :         memset(gen, 0, sz);
    8688              : 
    8689           10 :         gen->name = guc_strdup(ERROR, name);
    8690           10 :         gen->context = context;
    8691           10 :         gen->group = CUSTOM_OPTIONS;
    8692           10 :         gen->short_desc = short_desc;
    8693           10 :         gen->long_desc = long_desc;
    8694           10 :         gen->flags = flags;
    8695           10 :         gen->vartype = type;
    8696              : 
    8697           10 :         return gen;
    8698              : }

    8700              : /*
    8701              :  * Common code for DefineCustomXXXVariable subroutines: insert the new
    8702              :  * variable into the GUC variable array, replacing any placeholder.
    8703              :  */
    8704              : static void
    8705           10 : define_custom_variable(struct config_generic *variable)
    8706              : {
    8707           10 :         const char *name = variable->name;
    8708           10 :         const char **nameAddr = &name;
    8709              :         struct config_string *pHolder;
    8710              :         struct config_generic **res;
    8711              : 
    8712              :         /*
    8713              :          * See if there's a placeholder by the same name.
    8714              :          */
    8715           10 :         res = (struct config_generic **) bsearch((void *) &nameAddr,
    8716              :                                                                                          (void *) guc_variables,
    8717              :                                                                                          num_guc_variables,
    8718              :                                                                                          sizeof(struct config_generic *),
    8719              :                                                                                          guc_var_compare);
    8720           10 :         if (res == NULL)
    8721              :         {
    8722              :                 /*

    8724              :                  * make sure it's initialized to its default value.
    8725              :                  */
    8726           10 :                 InitializeOneGUCOption(variable);
    8727           10 :                 add_guc_variable(variable, ERROR);
    8728           10 :                 return;
    8729              :         }
    8730              : 
    8731              :         /*
    8732              :          * This better be a placeholder
    8733              :          */
    8734            0 :         if (((*res)->flags & GUC_CUSTOM_PLACEHOLDER) == 0)
    8735            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    8736              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INTERNAL_ERROR),
    8737              :                                  errmsg("attempt to redefine parameter \"%s\"", name)));
    8738              : 
    8739              :         Assert((*res)->vartype == PGC_STRING);
    8740            0 :         pHolder = (struct config_string *) (*res);
    8741              : 
    8742              :         /*
    8743              :          * First, set the variable to its default value.  We must do this even
    8744              :          * though we intend to immediately apply a new value, since it's possible
    8745              :          * that the new value is invalid.
    8746              :          */
    8747            0 :         InitializeOneGUCOption(variable);
    8748              : 
    8749              :         /*
    8750              :          * Replace the placeholder. We aren't changing the name, so no re-sorting
    8751              :          * is necessary
    8752              :          */
    8753            0 :         *res = variable;
    8754              : 
    8755              :         /*
    8756              :          * Assign the string value(s) stored in the placeholder to the real
    8757              :          * variable.  Essentially, we need to duplicate all the active and stacked
    8758              :          * values, but with appropriate validation and datatype adjustment.
    8759              :          *
    8760              :          * If an assignment fails, we report a WARNING and keep going.  We don't
    8761              :          * want to throw ERROR for bad values, because it'd bollix the add-on
    8762              :          * module that's presumably halfway through getting loaded.  In such cases
    8763              :          * the default or previous state will become active instead.
    8764              :          */
    8765              : 
    8766              :         /* First, apply the reset value if any */

    8768            0 :                 (void) set_config_option(name, pHolder->reset_val,
    8769              :                                                                  pHolder->gen.reset_scontext,
    8770              :                                                                  pHolder->gen.reset_source,
    8771              :                                                                  GUC_ACTION_SET, true, WARNING, false);
    8772              :         /* That should not have resulted in stacking anything */
    8773              :         Assert(variable->stack == NULL);
    8774              : 
    8775              :         /* Now, apply current and stacked values, in the order they were stacked */

    8777            0 :                                                    *(pHolder->variable),
    8778              :                                                    pHolder->gen.scontext, pHolder->gen.source);
    8779              : 
    8780              :         /* Also copy over any saved source-location information */

    8782            0 :                 set_config_sourcefile(name, pHolder->gen.sourcefile,
    8783              :                                                           pHolder->gen.sourceline);
    8784              : 
    8785              :         /*
    8786              :          * Free up as much as we conveniently can of the placeholder structure.
    8787              :          * (This neglects any stack items, so it's possible for some memory to be

    9196              : /*
    9197              :  * Return an array of modified GUC options to show in EXPLAIN.

    9200              :  * with values different from their built-in defaults.

    9203            0 : get_explain_guc_options(int *num)

    9229              :

    9235            0 :                         case PGC_BOOL:
    9236              :                                 {
    9237            0 :                                         struct config_bool *lconf = (struct config_bool *) conf;
    9238              : 
    9239            0 :                                         modified = (lconf->boot_val != *(lconf->variable));

    9242              : 
    9243            0 :                         case PGC_INT:

    9246              :

    9296              : 
    9297              : /*
    9298              :  * Return GUC variable value by name; optionally return canonical form of
    9299              :  * name.  If the GUC is unset, then throw an error unless missing_ok is true,
    9300              :  * in which case return NULL.  Return value is palloc'd (but *varname isn't).
    9301              :  */
    9302              : char *
    9303            1 : GetConfigOptionByName(const char *name, const char **varname, bool missing_ok)
    9304              : {
    9305              :         struct config_generic *record;
    9306              : 
    9307            1 :         record = find_option(name, false, ERROR);
    9308            1 :         if (record == NULL)
    9309              :         {
    9310            0 :                 if (missing_ok)
    9311              :                 {
    9312            0 :                         if (varname)
    9313            0 :                                 *varname = NULL;
    9314            0 :                         return NULL;
    9315              :                 }

    9739              : /*
    9740              :  * show_all_file_settings
    9741              :  *
    9742              :  * Returns a table of all parameter settings in all configuration files
    9743              :  * which includes the config file pathname, the line number, a sequence number
    9744              :  * indicating the order in which the settings were encountered, the parameter
    9745              :  * name and value, a bool showing if the value could be applied, and possibly
    9746              :  * an associated error message.  (For problems such as syntax errors, the
    9747              :  * parameter name/value might be NULL.)
    9748              :  *
    9749              :  * Note: no filtering is done here, instead we depend on the GRANT system
    9750              :  * to prevent unprivileged users from accessing this function or the view

    9752              :  */
