    9968              : 
    9969              : #ifdef EXEC_BACKEND
    9970              : 
    9971              : /*
    9972              :  *      These routines dump out all non-default GUC options into a binary
    9973              :  *      file that is read by all exec'ed backends.  The format is:
    9974              :  *
    9975              :  *              variable name, string, null terminated
    9976              :  *              variable value, string, null terminated
    9977              :  *              variable sourcefile, string, null terminated (empty if none)
    9978              :  *              variable sourceline, integer
    9979              :  *              variable source, integer
    9980              :  *              variable scontext, integer
    9981              :  */
    9982              : static void
    9983              : write_one_nondefault_variable(FILE *fp, struct config_generic *gconf)
    9984              : {
    9985              :         if (gconf->source == PGC_S_DEFAULT)
    9986              :                 return;
    9987              : 
    9988              :         fprintf(fp, "%s", gconf->name);
    9989              :         fputc(0, fp);
    9990              : 
    9991              :         switch (gconf->vartype)
    9992              :         {
    9993              :                 case PGC_BOOL:
    9994              :                         {
    9995              :                                 struct config_bool *conf = (struct config_bool *) gconf;
    9996              : 
    9997              :                                 if (*conf->variable)
    9998              :                                         fprintf(fp, "true");
    9999              :                                 else
   10000              :                                         fprintf(fp, "false");
   10001              :                         }
   10002              :                         break;
   10003              : 
   10004              :                 case PGC_INT:
   10005              :                         {
   10006              :                                 struct config_int *conf = (struct config_int *) gconf;
   10007              : 
   10008              :                                 fprintf(fp, "%d", *conf->variable);
   10009              :                         }
   10010              :                         break;
   10011              : 
   10012              :                 case PGC_REAL:
   10013              :                         {
   10014              :                                 struct config_real *conf = (struct config_real *) gconf;
   10015              : 
   10016              :                                 fprintf(fp, "%.17g", *conf->variable);
   10017              :                         }
   10018              :                         break;
   10019              : 
   10020              :                 case PGC_STRING:
   10021              :                         {
   10022              :                                 struct config_string *conf = (struct config_string *) gconf;
   10023              : 
   10024              :                                 if (*conf->variable)
   10025              :                                         fprintf(fp, "%s", *conf->variable);
   10026              :                         }
   10027              :                         break;
   10028              : 
   10029              :                 case PGC_ENUM:
   10030              :                         {
   10031              :                                 struct config_enum *conf = (struct config_enum *) gconf;
   10032              : 
   10033              :                                 fprintf(fp, "%s",
   10034              :                                                 config_enum_lookup_by_value(conf, *conf->variable));
   10035              :                         }
   10036              :                         break;
   10037              :         }
   10038              : 
   10039              :         fputc(0, fp);
   10040              : 
   10041              :         if (gconf->sourcefile)
   10042              :                 fprintf(fp, "%s", gconf->sourcefile);
   10043              :         fputc(0, fp);
   10044              : 
   10045              :         fwrite(&gconf->sourceline, 1, sizeof(gconf->sourceline), fp);
   10046              :         fwrite(&gconf->source, 1, sizeof(gconf->source), fp);
   10047              :         fwrite(&gconf->scontext, 1, sizeof(gconf->scontext), fp);
   10048              : }
   10049              : 
   10050              : void
   10051              : write_nondefault_variables(GucContext context)
   10052              : {
   10053              :         int                     elevel;
   10054              :         FILE       *fp;
   10055              :         int                     i;
   10056              : 
   10057              :         Assert(context == PGC_POSTMASTER || context == PGC_SIGHUP);
   10058              : 
   10059              :         elevel = (context == PGC_SIGHUP) ? LOG : ERROR;
   10060              : 
   10061              :         /*
   10062              :          * Open file
   10063              :          */
   10064              :         fp = AllocateFile(CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS_NEW, "w");
   10065              :         if (!fp)
   10066              :         {
   10067              :                 ereport(elevel,
   10068              :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10069              :                                  errmsg("could not write to file \"%s\": %m",
   10070              :                                                 CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS_NEW)));
   10071              :                 return;
   10072              :         }
   10073              : 
   10074              :         for (i = 0; i < num_guc_variables; i++)
   10075              :         {
   10076              :                 write_one_nondefault_variable(fp, guc_variables[i]);
   10077              :         }
   10078              : 
   10079              :         if (FreeFile(fp))
   10080              :         {
   10081              :                 ereport(elevel,
   10082              :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10083              :                                  errmsg("could not write to file \"%s\": %m",
   10084              :                                                 CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS_NEW)));
   10085              :                 return;
   10086              :         }
   10087              : 
   10088              :         /*
   10089              :          * Put new file in place.  This could delay on Win32, but we don't hold
   10090              :          * any exclusive locks.
   10091              :          */
   10092              :         rename(CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS_NEW, CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS);
   10093              : }
   10094              : 
   10095              : 
   10096              : /*
   10097              :  *      Read string, including null byte from file
   10098              :  *
   10099              :  *      Return NULL on EOF and nothing read
   10100              :  */
   10101              : static char *
   10102              : read_string_with_null(FILE *fp)
   10103              : {
   10104              :         int                     i = 0,
   10105              :                                 ch,
   10106              :                                 maxlen = 256;
   10107              :         char       *str = NULL;
   10108              : 
   10109              :         do
   10110              :         {
   10111              :                 if ((ch = fgetc(fp)) == EOF)
   10112              :                 {
   10113              :                         if (i == 0)
   10114              :                                 return NULL;
   10115              :                         else
   10116              :                                 elog(FATAL, "invalid format of exec config params file");
   10117              :                 }
   10118              :                 if (i == 0)
   10119              :                         str = guc_malloc(FATAL, maxlen);
   10120              :                 else if (i == maxlen)
   10121              :                         str = guc_realloc(FATAL, str, maxlen *= 2);
   10122              :                 str[i++] = ch;
   10123              :         } while (ch != 0);
   10124              : 
   10125              :         return str;
   10126              : }
   10127              : 
   10128              : 
   10129              : /*
   10130              :  *      This routine loads a previous postmaster dump of its non-default
   10131              :  *      settings.
   10132              :  */
   10133              : void
   10134              : read_nondefault_variables(void)
   10135              : {
   10136              :         FILE       *fp;
   10137              :         char       *varname,
   10138              :                            *varvalue,
   10139              :                            *varsourcefile;
   10140              :         int                     varsourceline;
   10141              :         GucSource       varsource;
   10142              :         GucContext      varscontext;
   10143              : 
   10144              :         /*
   10145              :          * Assert that PGC_BACKEND/PGC_SU_BACKEND case in set_config_option() will
   10146              :          * do the right thing.
   10147              :          */
   10148              :         Assert(IsInitProcessingMode());
   10149              : 
   10150              :         /*
   10151              :          * Open file
   10152              :          */
   10153              :         fp = AllocateFile(CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS, "r");
   10154              :         if (!fp)
   10155              :         {
   10156              :                 /* File not found is fine */
   10157              :                 if (errno != ENOENT)
   10158              :                         ereport(FATAL,
   10159              :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10160              :                                          errmsg("could not read from file \"%s\": %m",
   10161              :                                                         CONFIG_EXEC_PARAMS)));
   10162              :                 return;
   10163              :         }
   10164              : 
   10165              :         for (;;)
   10166              :         {
   10167              :                 struct config_generic *record;
   10168              : 
   10169              :                 if ((varname = read_string_with_null(fp)) == NULL)
   10170              :                         break;
   10171              : 
   10172              :                 if ((record = find_option(varname, true, FATAL)) == NULL)
   10173              :                         elog(FATAL, "failed to locate variable \"%s\" in exec config params file", varname);
   10174              : 
   10175              :                 if ((varvalue = read_string_with_null(fp)) == NULL)
   10176              :                         elog(FATAL, "invalid format of exec config params file");
   10177              :                 if ((varsourcefile = read_string_with_null(fp)) == NULL)
   10178              :                         elog(FATAL, "invalid format of exec config params file");
   10179              :                 if (fread(&varsourceline, 1, sizeof(varsourceline), fp) != sizeof(varsourceline))
   10180              :                         elog(FATAL, "invalid format of exec config params file");
   10181              :                 if (fread(&varsource, 1, sizeof(varsource), fp) != sizeof(varsource))
   10182              :                         elog(FATAL, "invalid format of exec config params file");
   10183              :                 if (fread(&varscontext, 1, sizeof(varscontext), fp) != sizeof(varscontext))
   10184              :                         elog(FATAL, "invalid format of exec config params file");
   10185              : 
   10186              :                 (void) set_config_option(varname, varvalue,
   10187              :                                                                  varscontext, varsource,
   10188              :                                                                  GUC_ACTION_SET, true, 0, true);
   10189              :                 if (varsourcefile[0])
   10190              :                         set_config_sourcefile(varname, varsourcefile, varsourceline);
   10191              : 
   10192              :                 free(varname);
   10193              :                 free(varvalue);
   10194              :                 free(varsourcefile);
   10195              :         }
   10196              : 
   10197              :         FreeFile(fp);
   10198              : }
   10199              : #endif                                                  /* EXEC_BACKEND */
   10200              : 
   10201              : /*
   10202              :  * can_skip_gucvar:
   10203              :  * When serializing, determine whether to skip this GUC.  When restoring, the
   10204              :  * negation of this test determines whether to restore the compiled-in default
   10205              :  * value before processing serialized values.
   10206              :  *
   10207              :  * A PGC_S_DEFAULT setting on the serialize side will typically match new
   10208              :  * postmaster children, but that can be false when got_SIGHUP == true and the
   10209              :  * pending configuration change modifies this setting.  Nonetheless, we omit
   10210              :  * PGC_S_DEFAULT settings from serialization and make up for that by restoring
   10211              :  * defaults before applying serialized values.
   10212              :  *
   10213              :  * PGC_POSTMASTER variables always have the same value in every child of a
   10214              :  * particular postmaster.  Most PGC_INTERNAL variables are compile-time
   10215              :  * constants; a few, like server_encoding and lc_ctype, are handled specially
   10216              :  * outside the serialize/restore procedure.  Therefore, SerializeGUCState()
   10217              :  * never sends these, and RestoreGUCState() never changes them.
   10218              :  */
   10219              : static bool
   10220            0 : can_skip_gucvar(struct config_generic *gconf)
   10221              : {
   10222            0 :         return gconf->context == PGC_POSTMASTER ||
   10223            0 :                 gconf->context == PGC_INTERNAL ||
   10224            0 :                 gconf->source == PGC_S_DEFAULT;
   10225              : }
   10226              : 
   10227              : /*
   10228              :  * estimate_variable_size:
   10229              :  *              Compute space needed for dumping the given GUC variable.
   10230              :  *
   10231              :  * It's OK to overestimate, but not to underestimate.
   10232              :  */
   10233              : static Size
   10234            0 : estimate_variable_size(struct config_generic *gconf)
   10235              : {

   10239            0 :         if (can_skip_gucvar(gconf))

   10243            0 :         size = strlen(gconf->name) + 1;

   10245              :         /* Get the maximum display length of the GUC value. */

   10247              :         {
   10248            0 :                 case PGC_BOOL:
   10249              :                         {
   10250            0 :                                 valsize = 5;    /* max(strlen('true'), strlen('false')) */
   10251              :                         }
   10252            0 :                         break;
   10253              : 
   10254            0 :                 case PGC_INT:
   10255              :                         {
   10256            0 :                                 struct config_int *conf = (struct config_int *) gconf;
   10257              : 
   10258              :                                 /*
   10259              :                                  * Instead of getting the exact display length, use max
   10260              :                                  * length.  Also reduce the max length for typical ranges of
   10261              :                                  * small values.  Maximum value is 2147483647, i.e. 10 chars.
   10262              :                                  * Include one byte for sign.
   10263              :                                  */

   10265            0 :                                         valsize = 3 + 1;
   10266              :                                 else

   10268              :                         }
   10269            0 :                         break;
   10270              : 
   10271            0 :                 case PGC_REAL:

   10281            0 :                         break;
   10282              : 
   10283            0 :                 case PGC_STRING:

   10298              : 
   10299            0 :                 case PGC_ENUM:

   10327              : /*
   10328              :  * EstimateGUCStateSpace:
   10329              :  * Returns the size needed to store the GUC state for the current process

   10331              : Size
   10332            0 : EstimateGUCStateSpace(void)
   10333              : {
   10334              :         Size            size;
   10335              :         int                     i;
   10336              : 
   10337              :         /* Add space reqd for saving the data size of the guc state */
   10338            0 :         size = sizeof(Size);
   10339              : 
   10340              :         /* Add up the space needed for each GUC variable */

   10343            0 :                                                 estimate_variable_size(guc_variables[i]));

   10348              : /*
   10349              :  * do_serialize:
   10350              :  * Copies the formatted string into the destination.  Moves ahead the
   10351              :  * destination pointer, and decrements the maxbytes by that many bytes. If
   10352              :  * maxbytes is not sufficient to copy the string, error out.
   10353              :  */
   10354              : static void
   10355            0 : do_serialize(char **destptr, Size *maxbytes, const char *fmt,...)
   10356              : {
   10357              :         va_list         vargs;
   10358              :         int                     n;
   10359              : 
   10360            0 :         if (*maxbytes <= 0)
   10361            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "not enough space to serialize GUC state");
   10362              : 
   10363            0 :         va_start(vargs, fmt);
   10364            0 :         n = vsnprintf(*destptr, *maxbytes, fmt, vargs);
   10365            0 :         va_end(vargs);
   10366              : 
   10367            0 :         if (n < 0)
   10368              :         {
   10369              :                 /* Shouldn't happen. Better show errno description. */
   10370            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "vsnprintf failed: %m with format string \"%s\"", fmt);
   10371              :         }
   10372            0 :         if (n >= *maxbytes)
   10373              :         {
   10374              :                 /* This shouldn't happen either, really. */
   10375            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "not enough space to serialize GUC state");
   10376              :         }
   10377              : 
   10378              :         /* Shift the destptr ahead of the null terminator */

   10382              :

   10620              : /*
   10621              :  * A little "long argument" simulation, although not quite GNU

   10624              :  * storage. Note that '-' is converted to '_' in the option name. If
   10625              :  * there is no '=' in the input string then value will be NULL.

   10628            8 : ParseLongOption(const char *string, char **name, char **value)

   10635              :         AssertArg(value);
   10636              : 
   10637            8 :         equal_pos = strcspn(string, "=");
   10638              : 
   10639            8 :         if (string[equal_pos] == '=')
   10640              :         {
   10641            8 :                 *name = guc_malloc(FATAL, equal_pos + 1);
   10642            8 :                 strlcpy(*name, string, equal_pos + 1);
   10643              : 
   10644            8 :                 *value = guc_strdup(FATAL, &string[equal_pos + 1]);
   10645              :         }
   10646              :         else
   10647              :         {
   10648              :                 /* no equal sign in string */
   10649            0 :                 *name = guc_strdup(FATAL, string);
   10650            0 :                 *value = NULL;
   10651              :         }
   10652              : 
   10653          142 :         for (cp = *name; *cp; cp++)
   10654          134 :                 if (*cp == '-')
   10655            0 :                         *cp = '_';

   10877              : /*

   10897            0 :         newarray = NULL;

   10945              : /*
   10946              :  * Validate a proposed option setting for GUCArrayAdd/Delete/Reset.
   10947              :  *
   10948              :  * name is the option name.  value is the proposed value for the Add case,
   10949              :  * or NULL for the Delete/Reset cases.  If skipIfNoPermissions is true, it's
   10950              :  * not an error to have no permissions to set the option.
   10951              :  *
   10952              :  * Returns true if OK, false if skipIfNoPermissions is true and user does not
   10953              :  * have permission to change this option (all other error cases result in an
   10954              :  * error being thrown).
   10955              :  */

   10957            0 : validate_option_array_item(const char *name, const char *value,

   10961              :         struct config_generic *gconf;
   10962              : 
   10963              :         /*
   10964              :          * There are three cases to consider:
   10965              :          *
   10966              :          * name is a known GUC variable.  Check the value normally, check
   10967              :          * permissions normally (i.e., allow if variable is USERSET, or if it's
   10968              :          * SUSET and user is superuser).
   10969              :          *
   10970              :          * name is not known, but exists or can be created as a placeholder (i.e.,
   10971              :          * it has a prefixed name).  We allow this case if you're a superuser,
   10972              :          * otherwise not.  Superusers are assumed to know what they're doing. We
   10973              :          * can't allow it for other users, because when the placeholder is
   10974              :          * resolved it might turn out to be a SUSET variable;
   10975              :          * define_custom_variable assumes we checked that.
   10976              :          *
   10977              :          * name is not known and can't be created as a placeholder.  Throw error,
   10978              :          * unless skipIfNoPermissions is true, in which case return false.
   10979              :          */
   10980            0 :         gconf = find_option(name, true, WARNING);
   10981            0 :         if (!gconf)
   10982              :         {
   10983              :                 /* not known, failed to make a placeholder */
   10984            0 :                 if (skipIfNoPermissions)
   10985            0 :                         return false;
   10986            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
   10987              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
   10988              :                                  errmsg("unrecognized configuration parameter \"%s\"",
   10989              :                                                 name)));
   10990              :         }
   10991              : 
   10992            0 :         if (gconf->flags & GUC_CUSTOM_PLACEHOLDER)
   10993              :         {
   10994              :                 /*
   10995              :                  * We cannot do any meaningful check on the value, so only permissions
   10996              :                  * are useful to check.
   10997              :                  */
   10998            0 :                 if (superuser())
   10999            0 :                         return true;
   11000            0 :                 if (skipIfNoPermissions)
   11001            0 :                         return false;
   11002            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
   11003              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
   11004              :                                  errmsg("permission denied to set parameter \"%s\"", name)));
   11005              :         }
   11006              : 
   11007              :         /* manual permissions check so we can avoid an error being thrown */
   11008            0 :         if (gconf->context == PGC_USERSET)
   11009              :                  /* ok */ ;

   11011              :                  /* ok */ ;
   11012            0 :         else if (skipIfNoPermissions)
   11013            0 :                 return false;

   11491              : static bool
   11492           10 : check_timezone_abbreviations(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
   11493              : {
   11494              :         /*
   11495              :          * The boot_val given above for timezone_abbreviations is NULL. When we
   11496              :          * see this we just do nothing.  If this value isn't overridden from the
   11497              :          * config file then pg_timezone_abbrev_initialize() will eventually
   11498              :          * replace it with "Default".  This hack has two purposes: to avoid
   11499              :          * wasting cycles loading values that might soon be overridden from the
   11500              :          * config file, and to avoid trying to read the timezone abbrev files
   11501              :          * during InitializeGUCOptions().  The latter doesn't work in an
   11502              :          * EXEC_BACKEND subprocess because my_exec_path hasn't been set yet and so
   11503              :          * we can't locate PGSHAREDIR.
   11504              :          */
   11505           10 :         if (*newval == NULL)
   11506              :         {
   11507              :                 Assert(source == PGC_S_DEFAULT);
   11508            5 :                 return true;
   11509              :         }
   11510              : 
   11511              :         /* OK, load the file and produce a malloc'd TimeZoneAbbrevTable */
   11512            5 :         *extra = load_tzoffsets(*newval);
   11513              : 
   11514              :         /* tzparser.c returns NULL on failure, reporting via GUC_check_errmsg */
   11515            5 :         if (!*extra)
   11516            0 :                 return false;
   11517              : 
   11518            5 :         return true;
   11519              : }
   11520              : 
   11521              : static void

   11527              :

   11531              : /*
   11532              :  * pg_timezone_abbrev_initialize --- set default value if not done already

   11801              : /*

   11803              :  * and certain whitespace chars are ignored, into a \0-separated (and
   11804              :  * \0\0-terminated) list of function names.  This formulation allows
   11805              :  * easy scanning when an error is thrown while avoiding the use of

   11807              :  * single palloc() chunk.

   11810            5 : check_backtrace_functions(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)

   11814              :         int                     validlen;
   11815              :         int                     i;
   11816              :         int                     j;
   11817              : 
   11818              :         /*
   11819              :          * Allow characters that can be C identifiers and commas as separators, as
   11820              :          * well as some whitespace for readability.
   11821              :          */
   11822            5 :         validlen = strspn(*newval,
   11823              :                                           "0123456789_"
   11824              :                                           "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
   11825              :                                           "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"
   11826              :                                           ", \n\t");
   11827            5 :         if (validlen != newvallen)
   11828              :         {
   11829            0 :                 GUC_check_errdetail("invalid character");

   11831              :         }
   11832              : 
   11833            5 :         if (*newval[0] == '\0')
   11834              :         {
   11835            5 :                 *extra = NULL;
   11836            5 :                 return true;
   11837              :         }
   11838              : 
   11839              :         /*

   11847            0 :                 if ((*newval)[i] == ',')

   11861            0 :         *extra = someval;
   11862            0 :         return true;
   11863              : }

   11911              : /*
   11912              :  * Recovery target settings: Only one of the several recovery_target* settings
   11913              :  * may be set.  Setting a second one results in an error.  The global variable
   11914              :  * recoveryTarget tracks which kind of recovery target was chosen.  Other
   11915              :  * variables store the actual target value (for example a string or a xid).
   11916              :  * The assign functions of the parameters check whether a competing parameter
   11917              :  * was already set.  But we want to allow setting the same parameter multiple
   11918              :  * times.  We also want to allow unsetting a parameter and setting a different
   11919              :  * one, so we unset recoveryTarget when the parameter is set to an empty
   11920              :  * string.
   11921              :  */

   11928              :                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   11929              :                          errmsg("multiple recovery targets specified"),
   11930              :                          errdetail("At most one of recovery_target, recovery_target_lsn, recovery_target_name, recovery_target_time, recovery_target_xid may be set.")));
   11931              : }

   11933              : static bool
   11934            5 : check_recovery_target(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)

   11940              :         }
   11941            5 :         return true;
   11942              : }
   11943              : 
   11944              : static void
   11945            5 : assign_recovery_target(const char *newval, void *extra)
   11946              : {
   11947            5 :         if (recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET &&
   11948            0 :                 recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE)
   11949            0 :                 error_multiple_recovery_targets();
   11950              : 
   11951            5 :         if (newval && strcmp(newval, "") != 0)
   11952            0 :                 recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE;
   11953              :         else
   11954            5 :                 recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET;
